stages.name: Milestone E Repro And CLI Authority

notes: Authority stays on scripts/ for the active smoke runtime.
Phase 5 adds a Python local web UI without changing keep-chain behavior.
Metrics stays optional and non-blocking exactly as established before Phase 5.
Repo-root src/scripts remains the compatibility zone until an explicit migration plan exists.
Authority stays on main_worktree/scripts.
Batch 7 already restored the retained validation baseline and repair authority as must-keep surfaces.
Batch 8 completed the repair-side archive closeout and Batch 9 canonicalized the stress shell surface.
Batch 10 closes the cleanup roadmap by fixing src/scripts as an operationally retained, exit-planned compatibility mirror.
Metrics stays optional and non-blocking exactly as established in Batch 6.
Control plane changes remain descriptive and must not alter keep-chain behavior.
Milestone E executes gate-first: contract locally, then parallel repro and delta work, then executor integration.
Milestone E is multi-market first with a compatibility layer for current ru-RU assets.
Phase 2 reviewer blockers were closed before status completion: staged candidate output, locale-grouped execution, and fail-closed glossary locale lookup.
Post-E roadmap is now interpreted as four execution phases while preserving milestone letters F-S.
Phase 2 first package is governance substrate hardening for run/session/milestone contracts and validation.
Phase 2 closeout extends the substrate with machine-checkable changed_files/evidence_refs/adr_refs and leaves Phase 3 in planning-ready only.
The milestone-I style-governance package is now merged as a bridge foundation on main, but it does not change the primary execution order.
Phase 1 large-batch runtime closure is now merged on main as PR #16 / commit 3a84f55.
Broader Phase 3 no longer needs to stay deferred, because H is now merged and the milestone-I bridge package is already on main.
Phase 3 language governance is now merged on main as the last completed post-E batch.
Current execution focus is the single Phase 4 operator-control-plane batch on codex/phase4-operator-control-plane-batch.
Phase 4 starts with bridge hardening on repair-loop target-column detection and explicit lifecycle-registry fail-closed behavior before the operator aggregation/report surfaces land.

phases.goal: Close qa/repair/review routing into one runtime-quality state machine.
Freeze run/session/evidence/milestone schemas before later operator surfaces expand.
Version style governance, human-review intake, feedback logging, lifecycle retirement, and KPI reporting.
Build an agent-first operator control plane, reporting surfaces, and final operating-model ADR.

validation_strategy.rationale: This phase batch closes hard QA repair, soft routing, rollback-safe promotion, and unified status semantics in the smoke orchestrator before broader Phase 3 resumes.
validation_strategy.acceptance_command: python -m py_compile scripts/run_smoke_pipeline.py && python -m pytest tests/test_batch6_repair_metrics_contract.py tests/test_phase1_quality_runtime_contract.py tests/test_repair_loop_contract.py tests/test_soft_qa_contract.py tests/test_smoke_verify.py -q && python -m pytest tests/test_translate_refresh_contract.py tests/test_milestone_e_e2e.py tests/test_plc_docs_contract.py -q && python scripts/plc_validate_records.py --preset representative --preset templates
validation_strategy.acceptance_summary: run_smoke_pipeline compiles; 31 passed on focused runtime tests; 21 passed on executor + PLC docs; PLC validator presets pass; PR #16 merged to main.

active_phase1_slice.next_action: keep Phase 1 as merged history only and move the active main scope to the Phase 3 batch

validation_strategy.rationale: This closeout package changes PLC/TriadDev governance contracts, validator rules, and representative lifecycle records only.
validation_strategy.acceptance_command: python -m pytest tests/test_plc_docs_contract.py -q && python scripts/plc_validate_records.py --preset representative --preset templates

active_phase2_slice.next_action: phase 2 PR merged; hand off to milestone_I_prepare planning-only work on clean main

explicit_non_goals: reopening Phase 1 runtime work except for fallout fixes

validation_strategy.rationale: Phase 3 changes runtime style-governance consumption, review/feedback persistence, lifecycle state, and KPI artifacts; one representative smoke path is required before merge.
validation_strategy.acceptance_command: python -m pytest tests/test_phase3_governance_helpers.py tests/test_phase3_runtime_governance.py tests/test_phase3_language_governance_contract.py tests/test_translate_refresh_contract.py tests/test_phase1_quality_runtime_contract.py tests/test_translate_style_contract.py tests/test_soft_qa_contract.py tests/test_plc_docs_contract.py -q && python scripts/style_sync_check.py && python scripts/plc_validate_records.py --preset representative --preset templates && python scripts/llm_ping.py
validation_strategy.acceptance_summary: 44 passed; style_sync_check pass; PLC validator presets pass; live smoke feasibility check is environment-blocked because the current shell lacks LLM_BASE_URL and LLM_API_KEY, so the required representative smoke gate is satisfied with deterministic orchestration coverage in tests/test_phase1_quality_runtime_contract.py.

active_phase3_slice.next_action: keep Phase 3 as merged history only and move the active main scope to the Phase 4 operator-control-plane batch

validation_strategy.rationale: Phase 4 aggregates existing artifacts into operator cards and reports; representative artifact-to-decision evidence is required, while live smoke is only needed if smoke orchestration semantics change again.

active_phase4_slice.next_action: finish bridge hardening, materialize operator cards/reports from the representative Phase 3 run, and open the single Phase 4 PR
